div1.entryFree: Circle, vb., to enclose, to surround: her breasts, like ivory globes --d with blue, Lucr. 407. the crimson blood --s her body in on every side, Lucr. 407 until thy head be --d with the same (sc. the diadem), H6B I, 2, 10. modest Dian --d with her nymphs, H6C IV, 8, 21. the imperial metal --ing now thy brow, R3 IV, 4, 382. her two branches (viz her arms) whose --ing shadows kings have sought to sleep in, Tit. II, 4, 19. you heavy people, c. me about, III, 1, 277. -- the maid with swelling drops gan wet her --d eyne, Lucr. 1229 (round eyes? or eyes surrounded with black circles?)
